evaluations.query: Brainstorm how we should design the notification system for our app.
expected_behavior: Detects project tier via codebase scanning before starting
Clarifies brainstorming constraints with AskUserQuestion before generating ideas
Creates TaskCreate with subtasks for all seven phases
Selects 3-5 relevant agents dynamically based on topic keywords
Generates 10+ divergent ideas before filtering
Runs feasibility fast-check including testability scoring
Synthesizes top 2-3 approaches with trade-off table and test strategy

evaluations.query: Compare these two approaches for our caching layer: Redis with TTL vs in-memory LRU cache.
expected_behavior: Detects this is a comparison mode, not open exploration
Skips divergent ideation phase since user already has specific approaches
Jumps to evaluation phase with the two provided options
Rates each approach on 6 dimensions including testability
Presents structured comparison with trade-offs and recommendation

evaluations.query: The login page throws a 500 error when I click submit. Fix it urgently.
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the brainstorming skill
Treats this as an urgent bug fix requiring immediate debugging
Uses standard debugging tools (Read, Grep, Bash) to investigate
Brainstorming is inappropriate for time-sensitive bug fixes

evaluations.query: Brainstorm a real-time collaboration feature but it must work offline-first, have zero added dependencies, fit within our 50KB JS budget, support IE11, and ship by next Friday.
expected_behavior: Acknowledges that constraints severely limit the solution space before divergent ideation
Generates ideas that respect all stated constraints rather than ignoring difficult ones
Flags constraints that are likely mutually exclusive such as real-time plus offline-first plus IE11
Presents a feasibility assessment that honestly rates which constraint combinations are achievable
Does not produce 10+ ideas that each violate one or more stated constraints

evaluations.query: Brainstorm a data storage approach. It must have zero latency, infinite storage, cost nothing, require no infrastructure, and guarantee 100% durability.
expected_behavior: Identifies that the constraints are physically impossible to satisfy simultaneously
Does not generate ideas pretending all constraints can be met at once
Asks the user to prioritize which constraints are hard requirements versus nice-to-haves
Suggests realistic tradeoff dimensions such as latency versus cost or durability versus infrastructure
